text: Thanks for chatting with me today. I'm really curious about how your team handles customer onboarding right now. Walk me through what happens when a new customer signs up. Sure. So when someone signs up, they get an automated welcome email, and then our customer success team reaches out within 24 hours. But honestly, it's a mess. The handoff between sales and CS is where things break down. What happens during that handoff? Sales closes the deal and puts notes in Salesforce, but half the time CS can't find the notes or they're incomplete. So CS ends up asking the customer the same questions again. How often does that happen? Honestly, almost every deal. It's embarrassing. We had a customer last week who said they felt like they were starting over. That's painful. What have you tried to fix it? We tried building a shared template in Google Docs, but nobody uses it consistently. We also tried having a sync meeting between sales and CS for every deal, but that didn't scale. If you could wave a magic wand, what would the ideal handoff look like? I'd want everything automatically transferred. The key requirements, the pain points discussed, the timeline, who the stakeholders are. And I'd want CS to have context before they even pick up the phone. How much time does your team waste on this currently? Each CS rep probably spends 2-3 hours a week just reconstructing deal context. With 8 reps, that's almost 20 hours a week. And that doesn't count the customer experience impact. Would you be open to doing a pilot with a couple of your CS reps to test a solution? Absolutely. I could get Sarah and Mike to try it out next month. They handle our biggest accounts so they feel this pain the most. That would be really helpful. Could you also introduce me to your head of sales so I can understand their side? Sure, I'll email an intro to Dave. He's been complaining about this too.
